    Features

    On this page

    • Voice-controlled conversational interface
    • Live deck monitoring & vision
    • Inventory tracking
    • Protocol library with LLM-ready metadata
    • Walk-up protocol design
    • Built for real labs

    CamBio brings a conversational, vision-aware layer to the OpenTrons Flex. Here’s what it does today.

    Voice-controlled conversational interface

    Talk to the robot the way you’d talk to a labmate. CamBio listens, asks clarifying questions, confirms its understanding, and speaks its plan aloud before acting.

    • Speech-to-text and text-to-speech running locally on Apple Silicon
    • Multiple selectable voices with low generation latency
    • A natural flow: greet → clarify → confirm → run
    • Text-only mode available for quiet environments and testing

    Live deck monitoring & vision

    A camera trained on the deck keeps CamBio aware of what’s actually there.

    • On-demand deck snapshots and a live video stream
    • A vision pipeline that identifies labware and detects tip presence
    • Empty-rack and tip-strip triage for fast, reliable reads
    • User recognition to tell new visitors from returning researchers

    Inventory tracking

    An independent, always-on model of your labware — on the deck and in storage.

    • Tip-level tracking: which positions are present or used in every rack
    • Tube and well contents: liquid identity and volume
    • Confidence values that decay over time so stale state gets re-verified
    • Visual deck editor and spreadsheet views for quick corrections

    Protocol library with LLM-ready metadata

    A growing, searchable catalog of working protocols designed for reuse.

    • Rich metadata: techniques, labware, pipettes, category, complexity, status
    • Indexed, organized library spanning many liquid-handling techniques
    • Built so an assistant can pull relevant examples as few-shot references
    • Search and retrieval surfaced directly in the conversational interface

    Walk-up protocol design

    The headline workflow: describe an experiment and let CamBio build it.

    1. Asks clarifying questions — volumes, inputs, outputs, labware
    2. Checks the current deck state and available equipment
    3. Designs a protocol, referencing similar examples from the library
    4. Saves it with validation (labware, syntax, metadata)
    5. Performs a dry run, narrating each move before any liquid is handled
    6. Executes after your spoken OK — then updates inventory

    Built for real labs

    • Multi-robot support with configurable deck layouts
    • Gripper-aware: knows when a protocol needs the Flex gripper and whether it’s attached
    • Session logging for reproducibility and debugging

    See the roadmap →
